user.title: Presence Simulator
user.description:
This integration learns how your home normally uses lights and energy through the week, then replays a realistic, randomized version while you're away.

Pick what to control while away (and optionally extra things to learn from). The two settings below shape *learning*; the day-to-day tuning controls (variation, quiet hours, timing, etc.) appear as adjustable controls on the device page once setup finishes — no need to decide them now.

data.controlled_entities: Entities to learn from and control when away simulation is enabled

data_description.controlled_entities: Lights and switches the simulator turns on and off while you're away to mimic someone being home. These are always learned from too, so you don't need to add them under 'Extra entities to learn from'.
data_description.monitored_entities: Extra things to learn from but not switch — e.g. motion or power/energy sensors, media players. Your controlled entities are already learned, so you don't need to list them here.
data_description.slot_minutes: How finely the week is divided when learning and replaying (e.g. 30-minute blocks). Smaller is more detailed but slower to learn. Changing this later starts a separate model for the new resolution (your old one is kept).
data_description.power_threshold: For power/energy sensors, the wattage above which a device counts as 'in use' while learning.

configure.title: Entities & learning
configure.description: Update what's controlled and learned from, and the two learning settings. Day-to-day tuning (variation, quiet hours, timing) lives on the device page as adjustable controls.

data.controlled_entities: Entities to learn from and control when away simulation is enabled
data.view_tuning_help: View what the tuning controls do

data_description.controlled_entities: Lights and switches the simulator turns on and off while away. These are always learned from too, so you don't need to add them under 'Extra entities to learn from'.
data_description.monitored_entities: Extra things to learn from but not switch. Your controlled entities are already learned, so you don't need to list them here.
data_description.slot_minutes: How finely the week is divided. Changing it starts a separate model for the new resolution; your current model is saved and restored if you switch back.
data_description.power_threshold: For power/energy sensors, the wattage above which a device counts as 'in use' while learning.
data_description.view_tuning_help: Turn this on and submit to open a guide explaining the day-to-day tuning controls. Your changes above are kept, and a Back button returns you here.

slot_confirm.title: Change schedule resolution?
slot_confirm.description:
Changing the schedule resolution switches to a separate learned model for the new resolution, so learning coverage will appear to start over.

Your current model is **saved**, not deleted — switch the resolution back and it's restored automatically. Submit to continue, or go back to keep the current resolution.

help.title: What the tuning controls do
help.description:
These live on the device page (Settings → Devices & Services → Presence Simulator) and can be changed anytime — they don't affect learned data.

- **Routine variation** — how often the house breaks its learned routine. 0% = faithful replay; higher = more surprises. Default 15%.
- **Minimum time between changes** — the shortest time a device holds its new state before changing again; stops lights flickering on and off. Default 20 min.
- **Switch timing jitter** — each change is nudged by a random delay up to this many minutes, so switches don't all happen on the clock tick. Default 7 min.
- **Max devices on at once** — a cap on how much of the house is lit at the same time, as a share of your controlled devices. 85% = at most ~85% on together. Lower it if the whole house lighting up looks unrealistic.
- **Quiet hours start / end** — an overnight window kept mostly dark (the odd light may still come on).

Learning settings (Schedule resolution, Power 'active' threshold) live on the *Entities & learning* page instead, because they change how the model is built.

This is a quick reference — for the full explanation, examples, and everything else the integration does, please read the complete **[README on GitHub](https://github.com/jamesecc/ha-presence-sim#what-each-control-does)**.

reset_model.name: Reset learned model
reset_model.description: Discard all learned activity history and start learning from scratch.

run_step.name: Run a simulation step now
run_step.description: Immediately recompute and apply away-mode decisions for the current time slot.

export_model.name: Export model summary
export_model.description: Return a summary of the learned model as a service response.
